name: Databricks SDK
pricing_notes: SDK is free; pay for Databricks DBU (Data Bricks Units) consumption

auth.notes: PAT (Personal Access Token) or M2M OAuth; Azure AD for Azure Databricks; GCP/AWS service principals

performance.notes: REST API latency ~200ms; job submission async; cluster startup 1-5 minutes

agent_readiness.notes: Official Databricks Python SDK for workspace management. Covers clusters, jobs, notebooks, secrets, SQL warehouses, MLflow, Unity Catalog. Async job execution. Model serving endpoints. Databricks AI Functions (SQL-based LLM calls). Well-maintained by Databricks. Also Go/Java/JS SDKs available. Better than using REST API directly.

gotchas: Databricks platform subscription required — significant cost
SDK covers management plane; data plane requires PySpark/SQL Connector
Some operations have eventual consistency (job status polling needed)
Cluster management APIs async by nature
best_when: Automating Databricks workspace management (jobs, clusters, permissions)
Agents that orchestrate Databricks ML workflows programmatically
MLOps pipelines triggering Databricks jobs from external orchestrators
Organizations using Unity Catalog for data governance automation
avoid_when: Data processing itself (use PySpark or Databricks SQL Connector instead)
Simple Spark jobs not requiring Databricks-specific features
summary: The official Databricks Python SDK for workspace automation: clusters, jobs, notebooks, secrets, SQL warehouses, MLflow, and Unity Catalog. Type-annotated with async job execution support. Well-maintained by Databricks. Best for organizations automating Databricks platform management and MLOps pipelines. Covers the management plane; use PySpark/SQL Connector for data plane operations.
